title: Waveform temporal-tolerance matrix (ADR-030 D5, PR-3)
description: Two-tier tolerance input for waveform_score.py (ADR-030 D5 anti-gerrymandering): `defaults` is the pre-committed severity→ceiling matrix (locked BEFORE seeing any divergence); `overrides` are the per-alert-class negotiated exceptions — each MUST carry justification + approved_by (audit trail) and the tool enforces tolerance_s <= the declared severity row's ceiling. `carve_outs` lists compliance fault-classes excluded from the catch-rate denominator (dual-control human review path, D1/OQ1). `ignored_unattributed` is the drain-then-shadow allowlist of aggregate alertnames whose presence in unattributed_alerts must NOT shadow no-hit cases into INDETERMINATE (persistent-noise drain so real misses surface as FN) — each MUST carry justification + approved_by (audit trail); an unlisted unattributed alert still shadows (unknown noise stays INDETERMINATE, the CRITICAL defence is preserved). Concrete tolerance values are engagement inputs — none are documented here (R2-2).

defaults.description: severity → tolerance ceiling in seconds. The `default` key is REQUIRED — it is the fallback row for alerts without a severity label (and for unknown severities).

severity.description: Declared ceiling row (must be a key of `defaults`). The tool enforces tolerance_s <= defaults[severity] at load time, and fails loud at scoring time if the live alert's severity label contradicts the declaration.

carve_outs.description: Compliance fault-classes (the waveform pack signature's `fault_class` slug — the case-side identity that exists BEFORE any alert fires) excluded from the catch-rate denominator and listed separately for dual-control review.

ignored_unattributed.description: Drain-then-shadow allowlist: aggregate `alertname`s whose presence in a report's unattributed_alerts is drained BEFORE the shadow test, so a persistent known-noise aggregate alert can no longer mask real misses into perpetual INDETERMINATE. Each entry MUST carry justification + approved_by (audit trail; enforced in code as well as here). CRITICAL invariant preserved: only the listed alertnames are drained — any unlisted unattributed alert still shadows no-hit cases into INDETERMINATE (unknown noise never reopens as PASS), and draining every unattributed alert lifts the shadow so genuine misses surface as FN (never washed to PASS).
